skill.name: cellosaurus-api
skill.description: Access Cellosaurus database for cell line information and release data. Invoke when user asks to search cell lines, get cell line details by accession, or check database release info.
skill.summary: API client for querying the Cellosaurus cell line database by accession number or search terms.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 52 static analysis findings are dismissed as false positives after AI evaluation. The 13 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' detections are pattern-matching errors against non-cryptographic code such as argparse descriptions and Markdown file extensions. The 28 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are Markdown code fence backticks (```), not shell command substitution operators. The 4 'System reconnaissance' findings refer to legitimate documentation about output discipline and audit file field names. The skill is a legitimate, well-documented API client for the public Cellosaurus database using only Python standard library urllib. No evidence of obfuscation, data exfiltration, credential theft, or prompt injection was found.

content.user_title: Query and Search the Cellosaurus Cell Line Database
content.value_statement: Access the Cellosaurus database to retrieve cell line information, search by identifiers, and check release data. This skill provides a command-line interface to query one of the most comprehensive cell line resources for biomedical research.
actual_capabilities: Search cell lines by identifier, name, or custom Solr query syntax
Retrieve detailed cell line information using CVCL accession numbers
Check Cellosaurus database release version and metadata
Request structured output in JSON, XML, TXT, or TSV format
Limit search results and select specific fields for targeted queries
Sort search results by custom fields and ordering
limitations: Requires an active internet connection to access the Cellosaurus API
Does not support cell line data submission or modification
Query syntax must follow Cellosaurus Solr conventions for complex searches
Cannot authenticate or access restricted database features

use_cases.title: Researching Cell Line Characteristics
use_cases.description: A biologist retrieves detailed information about a specific cell line using its CVCL accession number to verify its origin, disease association, and species.

use_cases.title: Finding Cell Lines by Search Criteria
use_cases.description: A researcher searches for all HeLa-derived cell lines to identify available variants for their experiment planning.

use_cases.title: Validating Database Currency
use_cases.description: A quality assurance specialist checks the latest Cellosaurus release version before starting a new analysis project to ensure data freshness.

prompt_templates.title: Get Cell Line Details
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the cellosaurus-api skill to get details for cell line CVCL_0030 (HeLa). Show the basic information including identifier, accession number, and species.
prompt_templates.scenario: Looking up a known cell line by its accession number

prompt_templates.title: Search Cell Lines by Keyword
prompt_templates.prompt: Search the Cellosaurus database for cell lines related to breast cancer. Use the search command and return the first 5 results with their identifiers and accession numbers.
prompt_templates.scenario: Finding relevant cell lines for a research topic

prompt_templates.title: Check Database Release Info
prompt_templates.prompt: Check the latest Cellosaurus release information using the cellosaurus-api skill. Show the release version and date.
prompt_templates.scenario: Verifying database version before starting analysis

prompt_templates.title: Export Cell Line Data in Specific Format
prompt_templates.prompt: Get details for cell line CVCL_0029 (A549) from the Cellosaurus database. Export the results in XML format with fields id, ac, and sy.
prompt_templates.scenario: Requesting structured output for downstream data processing

output_examples.input: Get information for cell line CVCL_0030
output_examples.output: Retrieved Cellosaurus record for CVCL_0030 (HeLa). The record includes the cell line identifier, accession number, species (Homo sapiens), and disease association (cervical adenocarcinoma). Results returned in JSON format.

output_examples.input: Search for cell lines related to lung cancer
output_examples.output: Found 12 cell lines matching the search criteria. Results include A549 (CVCL_0023), NCI-H1299 (CVCL_0060), and NCI-H460 (CVCL_0459). Each entry shows the identifier, accession number, and species.
Cellosaurus release version 2025-03-15 retrieved. The database contains information on over 250,000 cell lines from diverse species and tissue types. Release notes are available for review.

best_practices: Always verify the accession number format (CVCL_XXXX) before querying to avoid invalid request errors.
Use the --fields parameter to limit response size when you only need specific attributes of a cell line.
Check release info first to confirm you are working with the most current version of the database.
anti_patterns: Do not use free-text queries without specifying field prefixes like id: or ac: as this may return unexpected results.
Do not ignore the --rows parameter default of 10 when searching broad terms, as results may be incomplete.
Do not reuse cell line data without checking the database release date, as identifiers and classifications may change between releases.

faq.question: What is the Cellosaurus database?
faq.answer: Cellosaurus is a curated database that describes cell lines used in biomedical research. It includes information on species, tissue origin, disease association, and genetic modifications.

faq.question: What is a CVCL accession number?
faq.answer: A CVCL number is a unique identifier assigned to each cell line in the Cellosaurus database. For example, HeLa cells have the accession CVCL_0030.

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: No. The Cellosaurus public API does not require authentication. The skill accesses the free public endpoint at api.cellosaurus.org.

faq.question: What output formats are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports JSON, XML, TXT, and TSV output formats. JSON is the default format.

faq.question: Can I add or modify cell line data using this skill?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides read-only access to the Cellosaurus database. Data submission must be done through the official Cellosaurus website.

faq.question: What kind of search queries can I use?
faq.answer: You can search using Solr syntax with field prefixes like id:HeLa, ac:CVCL_0030, or species:Homo sapiens. The Cellosaurus API documentation has full query syntax details.
